title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers game state, scenes, pause, life loss, restart, and ownership into a changed explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states.
Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.
Freeze simulation clocks and dangerous input while keeping menus, status, audio control, and window events responsive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab State Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 8559 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab State Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab State Model, keep State Model connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Scene Evidence
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 6940 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python State Model · changed case · Lab State Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 2797 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-scene-evidence-w5y5av" that uses case 1428 to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-scene-evidence-w5y5av
# Competency: asteroids-scene-evidence.
# Case 1428: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_scene_evidence_w5y5av(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_w5y5av = "w5y5av-va5y5w-1428"
    assert evidence_variant_w5y5av
    evidence_axes_w5y5av = (
        "playerw5y5av",
        "seed1428",
        "inputva5y5w",
        "framew5y5av1428",
        "state1428w5y5av",
        "playtestva5y5w1428",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_w5y5av)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"
